text: Deep water running for 4 weeks can maintain 5-km race performance in trained competitive runners with no significant change in finish time.
Four weeks of deep water running maintains maximal oxygen consumption (VO2max) in trained runners.
Running economy (submaximal oxygen consumption at a fixed workload) is preserved after 4 weeks of exclusive deep water run training.
Lactate threshold running velocity is maintained after 4 weeks of deep water running replacing all on-land training.
A deep water running frequency of 5-6 sessions per week (20-24 total sessions over 4 weeks) is sufficient to maintain on-land running fitness.
Deep water running as a complete substitute for land-based training does not negatively affect mood states over a 4-week period.
